subject: Geography
title: The Olm: Europe's Cave Dragon
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Olm: Europe's Cave Dragon usable.

hook.text: The olm is a pale, blind salamander that lives in Slovenian caves and can live to 100 years.

buildup.text: Medieval people thought olms were baby dragons washed out of caves by floods.

discovery.text: Olms can survive without food for up to 10 years by slowing their metabolism to near zero.

twist.text: They sense prey using electrical fields—a skill more common in sharks than amphibians.

climax.text: The olm is a living relic, virtually unchanged for millions of years in its dark world.

punchline.text: A salamander that lives a century, eats once a decade, and hunts with electricity.

quiz.question: How long can an olm survive without food?
